summary: Company shares climbed by more than 20% on Phase 2 study results suggesting the therapy could provide sustained relief when administered once every three or six months.

title: Pfizer, Valneva to seek approval of Lyme disease vaccine despite mixed study results
summary: The partners said the shot produced “clinically meaningful” efficacy despite missing its main study objective, a finding they blamed on a lower-than-expected rate of infections during the trial.

title: Roche stops work on experimental SMA drug
summary: In a letter to European patients, Roche said its decision to end development of “emugrobart” was based on the drug “not consistently” improving muscle growth and motor function in a key study.

title: Novartis pays $2B to land a startup’s breast cancer drug
summary: Acquiring a unit of privately held Synnovation Therapeutics hands Novartis a prospect that might improve on medicines that, like its own Piqray, target tumors driven to growth by PIK3CA mutations.

title: Lilly’s three-pronged obesity drug hits goal in large diabetes trial
summary: Retatrutide, a possible advance on GLP-1 drugs like Zepbound, significantly cut blood sugar and body weight in the study, a sign it might be similarly impactful against obesity.

title: Stryker continues to bring hacked systems back online after patients delay surgeries
summary: Stryker said its manufacturing capacity is ramping up quickly as plants are brought back online as well as electronic ordering and shipping.

title: Novartis joins Lilly, AZ in China expansion with $480M commitment to boost manufacturing, R&D
summary: Novartis is investing more than 3.3 billion Chinese yuan (roughly $480 million) to soup up its manufacturing facility in Beijing and strengthen its R&D campus in Shanghai, where the company’s China headquarters are also located.

title: FDA solicits feedback on controversial national priority voucher review pathway
summary: The FDA has scheduled a public hearing for June 12 to hear input on the Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher program, which it introduced last June. So far, the program has resulted in four ultraspeedy drug reviews.

title: Sanofi returns to T-cell engager territory with $1.2B pact for phase 1-stage trispecific
summary: Sanofi has returned to T-cell engager territory via an agreement to pay $180 million in the near term for a phase 1-stage trispecific antibody from Kali Therapeutics.

title: Apogee eczema data drive hype to new peak as threat to Lilly and Sanofi becomes clear
summary: Apogee Therapeutics has published phase 2 eczema data that suggest its anti-IL-13 antibody is a threat to Eli Lilly, Regeneron and Sanofi, prompting analysts to double their peak sales forecast to $5.2 billion.

title: Avoiding 11th hour surprises in clinical trials

title: Pfizer, Valneva blame low Lyme cases for phase 3 vaccine fail but still plan approval push
summary: Pfizer and Valneva have blamed “fewer than anticipated” Lyme disease cases for the failure of their phase 3 trial to hit its key goal. But the companies still plan to file the vaccine with regulators.

title: Oryon shines out of stealth with $21M to fight Parkinson's disease
summary: When Ron Cohen, M.D., CEO of Oryon Cell Therapies, recently dug into the company’s Parkinson’s disease data, he told Fierce Biotech it “sang” to him. The former internist-turned-biotech CEO knew a thing or two about Parkinson’s—an area Oryon is targeting with an autologous neuron replacement therapy designed to restore dopaminergic function.

title: Genentech antibody fails to boost muscle in rare diseases, raising questions for obesity trial
summary: Genentech is halting development of an antibody for two rare genetic diseases after the candidate failed to boost muscle growth, raising questions about whether the molecule can preserve muscle in two still-ongoing obesity trials.

title: Imaging Interoperability Offers a Lifeline to Rural Hospitals and the Patients Depending on Them
summary:
Typically, rural hospitals operate as “islands,” where a patient might receive an initial scan at a community health facility, see a specialist at a regional medical center, and return home for follow-up care. In this scenario, it’s common for their medical images to become trapped in three different systems.
The post Imaging Interoperability Offers a Lifeline to Rural Hospitals and the Patients Depending on Them appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Why GI Providers Should Care About That “Health Hack”
summary:
Patients want to explore more holistic options and are becoming more educated and interested in their own health. However, this trend of growing trust in unchecked online health advice often introduces more risk to patients than help.

title: The Saga of Utah’s Rx Refill Bot: A Bold Bet on AI & Researchers Who Cried Foul
summary:
Utah is piloting an AI system developed by Doctronic to autonomously handle routine prescription refills, aiming to reduce delays and boost medication adherence. But a report from Mindgard AI claiming that there are vulnerabilities in the company’s chatbot underscores the broader challenge regulators and developers face in ensuring healthcare AI systems remain safe and reliable in real-world use.

title: Why Providence Wants to Sell Its Health Plan
summary:
